title: Subject-Verb Agreement
description: Master the fundamental rule of matching subjects with their correct verb forms. Learn when to use base verbs vs. S-form verbs.
learning_objectives: Understand the concept of grammatical person (1st, 2nd, 3rd)
Apply the Third-Person Singular rule (He/She + verb-s)

intro_content.title: The Golden Rule of English Verbs
intro_content.explanation: In English, when the subject is 'He', 'She', or 'It' (3rd person singular), the verb gets an '-s' or '-es' ending in present tense. All other subjects (I, You, We, They) use the base form.

challenges.context_description: A woman reaching for an apple on a tree
challenges.target_concept_explanation: When the subject is 'She' (3rd person singular), the verb must have the '-s' form. 'She' + 'grabs' = correct. 'She' + 'grab' = incorrect.
hints: The subject is 3rd person singular. What happens to the verb?

SUBJECT_SHE|GRAB|APPLE.feedback: Almost! 'She' is 3rd person singular, so the verb needs an '-s'. Use 'grabs' instead of 'grab'.
SUBJECT_SHE|GRAB|APPLE.rule_reference: 3rd person singular subjects (He/She/It) require S-form verbs in present tense.
SUBJECT_SHE|GRAB|APPLE.gesture_hint: Try the GRABS gesture instead of GRAB

SUBJECT_HE|GRABS|APPLE.feedback: Check the prompt again - the subject should be 'She', not 'He'.
SUBJECT_HE|GRABS|APPLE.rule_reference: Match your gesture to the subject shown in the prompt.
SUBJECT_HE|GRABS|APPLE.gesture_hint: Use the SHE gesture (different from HE)

SUBJECT_I|GRAB|APPLE.feedback: The prompt shows 'She', but you signed 'I'. Also, note that 'I' uses base form 'grab', not 'grabs'.
SUBJECT_I|GRAB|APPLE.rule_reference: Read the prompt carefully and match the subject.
SUBJECT_I|GRAB|APPLE.gesture_hint: The prompt specifies 'She' - use the appropriate gesture

SUBJECT_THEY|GRAB|APPLE.feedback: 'They' is plural, but the prompt shows 'She' (singular). Different subject!
SUBJECT_THEY|GRAB|APPLE.rule_reference: Singular subjects: I, You, He, She, It. Plural: We, They.

SUBJECT_SHE|GRABS.feedback: Good start! 'She grabs' is correct so far. But 'grabs' is transitive - it needs an object. What does she grab?
SUBJECT_SHE|GRABS.rule_reference: Transitive verbs require a direct object to complete the sentence.
SUBJECT_SHE|GRABS.gesture_hint: Add an object like APPLE to complete the sentence

GRABS|APPLE.feedback: You started with a verb! Every sentence needs a subject first. Who is doing the grabbing?
GRABS|APPLE.rule_reference: English sentences follow S-V-O order: Subject first, then Verb, then Object.
GRABS|APPLE.gesture_hint: Start with SUBJECT_SHE, then add the verb

challenges.context_description: Children playing with a ball in a park
challenges.target_concept_explanation: When the subject is 'They' (3rd person PLURAL), the verb uses the BASE form without '-s'. 'They' + 'grab' = correct. 'They' + 'grabs' = incorrect.
hints: 'They' refers to multiple people. Is it singular or plural?
Plural subjects use the BASE form of the verb.
Base form means no '-s': grab, want, eat (not grabs, wants, eats)

SUBJECT_THEY|GRABS|BALL.feedback: Careful! 'They' is PLURAL, so you need the base form 'grab', NOT 'grabs'. The '-s' form is only for singular He/She/It.
SUBJECT_THEY|GRABS|BALL.rule_reference: Only 3rd person SINGULAR (He/She/It) uses '-s'. Plural subjects (They/We) use base form.

SUBJECT_HE|GRABS|BALL.feedback: The prompt says 'They' (plural), but you signed 'He' (singular). Different subject needed!
SUBJECT_HE|GRABS|BALL.rule_reference: Match your gestures to the prompt. 'They' ≠ 'He'.
SUBJECT_HE|GRABS|BALL.gesture_hint: Use the THEY gesture to represent multiple people

SUBJECT_THEY|GRAB.feedback: Great job on 'They grab'! The verb form is correct. Now add an object - what do they grab?
SUBJECT_THEY|GRAB.rule_reference: Transitive verbs like 'grab' require an object.
SUBJECT_THEY|GRAB.gesture_hint: Add BALL to complete the sentence

SUBJECT_THEY|GOES.feedback: 'Goes' is the S-form, but 'They' needs base form 'go'. Also, 'go' is intransitive - it can't take an object like 'ball'.
SUBJECT_THEY|GOES.rule_reference: 1) They + base form. 2) 'Go' doesn't take objects.
SUBJECT_THEY|GOES.gesture_hint: Use GRAB (transitive, base form) for this sentence

SUBJECT_THEY|GO|BALL.feedback: 'Go' is intransitive - it cannot take 'ball' as an object. Use a transitive verb like 'grab' or 'want'.
SUBJECT_THEY|GO|BALL.rule_reference: Intransitive verbs (go, stop, sleep) don't take direct objects.

GRAB|BALL.feedback: You're missing the subject! Who grabs the ball? Start with 'They'.
GRAB|BALL.rule_reference: Every English sentence needs a subject.

key_takeaways: He/She/It (3rd person singular) → Verb + 's' (walks, grabs, eats)
This rule only applies to PRESENT TENSE
Always build sentences in S-V-O order
common_mistakes: Putting the object before the verb

achievements.title: Grammar Guardian
achievements.description: Complete all challenges without agreement errors

achievements.title: Quick Thinker
achievements.description: Complete both challenges under the time bonus threshold
